question: Does increasing token tile size make group-aware sparse dKV accumulation more valuable, and where should the first no-HSK query-major compact reducer start?

important_invariant.read: Increasing token_block creates more tokens per compressed group segment, but still at most one selected row per token per group segment. This means larger token tiles improve cross-token dKV write coalescing, not same-token selected-slot reuse. A group-owned contribution producer still loses q/dO reuse.

pack_timing_context.read: Route packing remains cheap enough at the established T32 gates. The missing primitive is not route metadata construction; it is a producer epilogue that reduces by group without serializing the MFMA contribution loop.

start_kernel_gate.reason: This is the first shape where almost all pairs participate in useful local reduction: pair_share_ge16 is 94.9%, p50 segment length is 23, and modeled write reduction is 23.8x. It is also the established route-pack/timing contract from prior probes.

second_gate_if_producer_remains_fast.reason: T64 doubles modeled write reduction to 47.3x and makes 95.2% of pairs sit in segments of at least 32, but it also doubles per-tile producer work/state. Only try after the T32 producer keeps query-major MFMA throughput.

do_not_start_at.reason: T128 gives the best modeled write reduction, but each tile has about 49k selected pairs and about 528 active group segments on average. That is too large for a first fused producer and risks recreating the scalar segment-owned failure at a bigger scale.

decision.next_implementation_cut: Implement T32 selected512 no-HSK query-major producer with tile-local segmented/grouped dKV epilogue. The kernel must keep q/dO hot and avoid group-owned recompute. Measure isolated S4096 latency first; only run 4x/8x e2e TFLOPs if it beats the current 42.54 ms no-HSK direct atomic whole-boundary path and trends toward the 8.0-8.8 ms compressed dKV atomic slice.

materialized_e2e_tflops.reason: This artifact is a route-shape decision, not a better latency candidate. No e2e TorchTitan A/B is justified until the T32 no-HSK compact reducer exists and passes the isolated latency gate.
